findings.title: labring/FastGPT
findings.description: FastGPT is a knowledge-based platform built on the LLMs, offers a comprehensive suite of out-of-the-box capabilities such as data processing, RAG retrieval, and visual AI workflow orchestration, letting you easily develop and deploy complex question-answering systems without the need for extensive setup or configuration.

findings.title: NirDiamant/RAG_Techniques
findings.description: This repository showcases various advanced techniques for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. Each technique has a detailed notebook tutorial.

findings.description: 📑 PageIndex: Document Index for Vectorless, Reasoning-based RAG

findings.title: deepset-ai/haystack
findings.description: Open-source AI orchestration framework for building context-engineered, production-ready LLM applications. Design modular pipelines and agent workflows with explicit control over retrieval, routing, memory, and generation. Built for scalable agents, RAG, multimodal applications, semantic search, and conversational systems.

findings.title: vanna-ai/vanna
findings.description: 🤖 Chat with your SQL database 📊. Accurate Text-to-SQL Generation via LLMs using Agentic Retrieval 🔄.

findings.title: memvid/memvid
findings.description: Memory layer for AI Agents. Replace complex RAG pipelines with a serverless, single-file memory layer. Give your agents instant retrieval and long-term memory.

findings.title: Tencent/WeKnora
findings.description: LLM-powered framework for deep document understanding, semantic retrieval, and context-aware answers using RAG paradigm.

findings.title: activeloopai/deeplake
findings.description: Deeplake is AI Data Runtime for Agents. It provides serverless postgres with a multimodal datalake, enabling scalable retrieval and training.

findings.title: OpenSPG/KAG
findings.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs.  It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.

findings.title: SciPhi-AI/R2R
findings.description: SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

findings.abstract: Image geolocalization has traditionally been addressed through retrieval-based place recognition or geometry-based visual localization pipelines. Recent advances in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated strong zero-shot reasoning capabilities across multimodal tasks, yet their performance

findings.title: BAGEL: Benchmarking Animal Knowledge Expertise in Language Models
findings.abstract: Large language models have shown strong performance on broad-domain knowledge and reasoning benchmarks, but it remains unclear how well language models handle specialized animal-related knowledge under a unified closed-book evaluation protocol. findings.title: Sketching the Readout of Large Language Models for Scalable Data Attribution and Valuation

findings.title: MARCH: Multi-Agent Radiology Clinical Hierarchy for CT Report Generation

findings.title: Beyond One-Size-Fits-All: Adaptive Test-Time Augmentation for Sequential Recommendation
findings.abstract: Test-time augmentation (TTA) has become a promising approach for mitigating data sparsity in sequential recommendation by improving inference accuracy without requiring costly model retraining. However, existing TTA methods typically rely on uniform, user-agnostic augmentation strategies. We show th

findings.title: DenTab: A Dataset for Table Recognition and Visual QA on Real-World Dental Estimates

findings.title: MMGait: Towards Multi-Modal Gait Recognition

findings.abstract: Despite the considerable promise of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), many real-world use cases may create privacy concerns, where the purported utility of RAG-enabled insights comes at the risk of exposing private information to either the LLM or the end user requesting the response. findings.title: Integrating Graphs, Large Language Models, and Agents: Reasoning and Retrieval
findings.abstract: Generative AI, particularly Large Language Models, increasingly integrates graph-based representations to enhance reasoning, retrieval, and structured decision-making. Despite rapid advances, there remains limited clarity regarding when, why, where, and what types of graph-LLM integrations are most

findings.title: RAGognizer: Hallucination-Aware Fine-Tuning via Detection Head Integration
findings.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is widely used to augment the input to Large Language Models (LLMs) with external information, such as recent or domain-specific knowledge. findings.title: Beyond the mass-radius plane: Integrated radiative-convective and interior structure simulations of the exoplanet continuum
findings.abstract: Static structure models, which map mass-radius constraints to bulk planet composition, are frequently used to categorise exoplanets due to their computational efficiency and the high-level insight they offer into planetary properties. findings.title: JFinTEB: Japanese Financial Text Embedding Benchmark
findings.abstract: We introduce JFinTEB, the first comprehensive benchmark specifically designed for evaluating Japanese financial text embeddings. findings.title: Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents
findings.abstract: As LLM agents scale to long-horizon, multi-session deployments, efficiently managing accumulated experience becomes a critical bottleneck. findings.title: TinyMU: A Compact Audio-Language Model for Music Understanding

findings.title: UsefulBench: Towards Decision-Useful Information as a Target for Information Retrieval
findings.abstract: Conventional information retrieval is concerned with identifying the relevance of texts for a given query. findings.title: CHOP: Chunkwise Context-Preserving Framework for RAG on Multi Documents
findings.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems lose retrieval accuracy when similar documents coexist in the vector database, causing unnecessary information, hallucinations, and factual errors. findings.title: Spectral design principles for local-excitation retention in impurity-assisted atomic arrays
findings.abstract: Enhanced local-excitation retention in atomic arrays allows to exploit cooperative radiative effects to suppress emission and prolong excited-state lifetimes. We consider an impurity-assisted setting involving a single storage atom being initially excited and study the survival of local excitation u

findings.title: Scattered Hypothesis Generation for Open-Ended Event Forecasting
findings.abstract: Despite the importance of open-ended event forecasting for risk management, current LLM-based methods predominantly target only the most probable outcomes, neglecting the intrinsic uncertainty of real-world events.
